messages.content: me and jordan broke up last weekend and i'm just kinda sad about it

I'm really sorry to hear that. Breakups are rough, especially when it's still so fresh - a week isn't very long at all.

Do you want to talk about it? Like what's going on in your head tonight, or just how you're feeling in general?
it was pretty mutual, nobody did anything terrible, we just wanted different things

Those can almost be harder in a weird way, you know? Like there's no one to be angry at, no clear villain - just this sad situation where two people who probably care about each other realize it's not going to work. That can leave you feeling kind of... lost, maybe?

How are you holding up day to day? Like are you doing okay or is it hitting you pretty hard?
still though, three years. i keep reaching for my phone to text them stuff and then remembering

Oh, that's such a specific kind of hurt. Three years means they were just *woven into* your daily life - all those little moments where your first instinct is still "I need to tell Jordan this." That reflex doesn't just switch off overnight.

It's almost like your habits haven't caught up with reality yet. Which makes sense, you can't just rewire three years in a week.

Is there a particular kind of moment that gets you the most? Like certain times of day, or specific things that make you reach for your phone?
i had a little cry tonight watching one of our old shows. felt good actually, like letting it out

That's actually really healthy - letting yourself feel it rather than pushing it down. And there's something kind of meaningful about that, watching a show that was *yours together* and just... letting the sadness be there.

A good cry can feel like a release valve sometimes, right? Like your body just needed to process some of it.

It sounds like you're being pretty gentle with yourself about all this, which is good. Some people try to just power through and pretend they're fine.
i'll be ok, i know i will. just gonna be a sad few weeks and that's normal i think

Yeah, that's exactly right. You've got a really grounded perspective on it - not pretending it doesn't hurt, but also trusting that you'll come out the other side. That's a healthy place to be.

And sad few weeks is probably about right for three years together. Give yourself permission to have those moments, like tonight with the show, without feeling like you need to rush past them.

You sound like you're handling it pretty well honestly, all things considered.
